q: Fossil fuels, such as coal, oil and natural gas, are considered renewable sources of energy.
Carbon is the main constituent of fossil fuels.
Fossil fuels store a large amount of kinetic energy.
The chemical energy stored in fossil fuels is converted into only one form of energy: heat.
Fossil fuels are not suitable for transportation needs due to their low energy density.
Crude oil extracted from the ground is directly used as gasoline without further processing.
Petrochemical products refined from fossil fuel liquids are used in manufacturing products from plastics to solvents.
Coal is formed through a process called liquefaction.
Coal is made of decomposed plant matter under conditions of high temperature and pressure.
Coal takes a relatively shorter amount of time to form compared to other fossil fuels.
Coal is a uniform substance with a consistent composition across all deposits.
Natural gas (methane) forms quickly and does not take nearly as long as other fossil fuels to form.
Methane is a common gas found in swamps.
Methane is solely a by-product of industrial processes, not animal digestive systems.
Nuclear energy is considered renewable because uranium fuels are easily replenishable after fission reactions.
Energy produced in a nuclear reactor can be harnessed to produce electricity.
Nuclear energy is used to power vessels like submarines and ships, allowing them to sail for long periods without refueling.
Radioisotopes, obtained as by-products in nuclear reactions, are used in medicine, agriculture and research.
All fossil fuels take millions of years to form, including natural gas.
Nuclear fission reactions lead to the replenishing of uranium fuels.
The chemical energy in fossil fuels is converted only into heat and light energy.
The fossil fuel industry refines crude oil into gasoline for transportation, but not into petrochemical products.
Coalification is the process responsible for forming natural gas.
Uranium fuels are consumed in nuclear fusion reactions, making them non-renewable.
Natural gas is found only in independent deposits within the ground.
